text.title: rag-eval-generation-metrics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rag-eval-generation-metrics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Always score all three RAG Triad dimensions together. A high Faithfulness score with low Context Relevance means the answer is faithful to irrelevant context — the system is confidently wrong. A high Answer Relevance with low Faithfulness means the answer sounds correct but is not grounded.
rule.rationale: Single-dimension scoring produces misleading conclusions. The three dimensions are designed to catch different failure modes in the same pipeline; all three must pass before a deployment quality gate can be considered passed.

rule.statement: RAGAS context_precision requires ground_truth in the dataset even though it measures retrieval quality (not generation). Always include ground_truth when constructing the RAGAS Dataset, even for retrieval-side metric computation.
rule.rationale: RAGAS naming is confusing: context_precision is a retrieval metric (how much of the retrieved context is relevant) but it is part of the RAGAS dataset contract that requires ground_truth. Missing this causes a silent failure or incorrect metric values.
